'''Ben Heaton''' (12 March 1990 – 22 June 2023) was an English professional [[rugby league]] player who played as a [[Rugby league positions#Centre|centre]], [[Rugby league positions#Second-row|second row]] or [[Rugby league positions#Full-back|fullback]] for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], [[Batley Bulldogs]] and [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]].

==Club career==
Heaton started his career with [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] before joining [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. While at Halifax, he spent time on loan with the [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref name="Match Report: Eagles Vs Batley">{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/News/Article/35414|title=Match Report: Eagles Vs Batley|publisher=Sheffield Eagles|date=28 July 2014|accessdate=26 September 2016|archive-date=1 March 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180301044721/http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/News/Article/35414|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Heaton recognised by ‘Rugby League World’">{{cite web|url=http://www.roughyeds.co.uk/news/heaton-recognised-by-rugby-league-world/|title=Heaton recognised by 'Rugby League World'|publisher=Oldham R.L.F.C.|date=9 August 2011|accessdate=26 September 2016|archive-date=1 March 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180301044715/http://www.roughyeds.co.uk/news/heaton-recognised-by-rugby-league-world/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In October 2018, Heaton signed a one-year deal with [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] club [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Hunslet pull off Ben Heaton coup |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hunslet-pull-off-ben-heaton-coup |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=23 June 2023 |date=26 October 2018}}</ref>

In September 2020, Heaton returned to former club [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=www.roughyeds.co.uk|date=30 Sep 2020|accessdate=26 Feb 2021|title=Ben Heaton Comes Home on Two Year Deal|url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2020/09/ben-heaton-comes-home-on-two-year-deal/|archive-date=16 January 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210116203359/https://roughyeds.co.uk/2020/09/ben-heaton-comes-home-on-two-year-deal/|url-status=dead}}</ref> In 2022, he joined local amateur club, Higginshaw.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ben Heaton now at Higgy |url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2022/05/ben-heaton-now-at-higgy/ |publisher=Oldham Rugby League Football Club |access-date=23 June 2023 |date=2 May 2022 |archive-date=26 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230626182818/https://roughyeds.co.uk/2022/05/ben-heaton-now-at-higgy/ |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Death==
On 23 June 2023, [[Halifax Panthers]] announced that Heaton had died on 23 June, at the age of 33. His cause of death has not been released.<ref>{{cite news |title=Ben Heaton: Halifax Panthers play tribute to former player who died last night, aged 33 |url=https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/people/ben-heaton-halifax-panthers-play-tribute-to-former-player-who-died-last-night-aged-33-4195240 |access-date=23 June 2023 |publisher=Halifax Courier |date=23 June 2023}}</ref>
